name: Session

pricing.details: Free and open source. Funded by the OPTF (Oxen Privacy Tech Foundation).

description: Decentralized messaging application requiring no phone number or email for account creation. Uses a randomly generated Session ID. Routes messages through a decentralized onion routing network.
rationale: Session's anonymous account creation model — no phone number, no email — removes a significant metadata risk present in Signal. The decentralized routing network eliminates central server dependencies. Forward secrecy properties are weaker than the Signal Protocol, and the application has not received a published comprehensive security audit. Usability is solid for a non-phone-number-based messenger.
